params.name: Bronze Head from Ife
params.description:
On Jan 4, 1268 AD, the Bronze Head from Ife was cast.
This uniquely shaped head comes from the Ife Kingdom in the 13th century and was made using a technique called lost wax casting. Its facial features are exquisitely realistic, and the shape is very distinctive, with a large number of African black carving-specific strip patterns on the face, showing considerable artistic expression, marking the peak of Nigerian art at that time.
